capability: signal-derived novelty reserve tracks recent novelty rate
mechanism: noveltyRateProfile — the γ-decayed count of newcomer atoms over prior steps, carried as the `novelty` amplitude through the FIXED Born step (surpriseAt). Context enters at the amplitude; the squaring/normalization law is unchanged. src/core/surprise.js + the opts.reserve='signal' path in src/perceiver/reading.js.
held: This file is the answer key. The measure script never reads it; the scorer reads it AFTER the measurement is written.

predicted.mechanism: On a NEWCOMER probe, signal.bayesBits(turnover) < signal.bayesBits(stable): after a burst of newcomers the reserve is high and a further newcomer is expected (belief barely moves); after a long stretch of confirmation the reserve is low and a newcomer is unexpected (belief moves far). Holds in BOTH senses (text + frequency) → interior.
predicted.control: On the SAME newcomer pair, const.bayesBits(turnover) == const.bayesBits(stable) to rounding: the constant reserve is blind to the turnover/stability of the signal's own history (the stimuli are built so sumPrior and probe-novelty are matched across the pair). For text, the surface channel surprisalBits(turnover) == surprisalBits(stable) too — same probe, same position.
predicted.null: On a CONFIRM probe, the reserve holds mass for an UNSEEN atom, so it does not move a confirmation: signal.bayesBits(turnover) ≈ signal.bayesBits(stable), and the confirm-pair separation is strictly smaller than the newcomer-pair separation. This rules out 'the signal reserve separates everything'.
predicted.instrument: reserveAmp(turnover) > reserveAmp(stable) on the matched prefixes — the amplitude is high after newcomers, low after confirmation, exactly the rate it should track.

verdict_rule: CONFIRMED iff: control caught (const blind on both newcomer pairs) AND mechanism fires in BOTH senses (signal turnover < stable) AND null holds (confirm separation < newcomer separation, both senses) AND every channel computed.
